title: Lines Traced Under An Image Of Amor Threatening
content:
Fear me, virgin whosoever
    Taking pride from love exempt,
    Fear me, slighted. Never, never
    Brave me, nor my fury tempt:
    Downy wings, but wroth they beat
    Tempest even in reason's seat.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Herman Melville, titled "Lines Traced Under An Image Of Amor Threatening", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
